english: Personally, I think we {should} look at both sides.
korean: 개인적으로, 양쪽 입장을 모두 봐야 한다고 생각해요.

english: Honestly, I'm not sure about the {plan} yet.
korean: 솔직히, 그 계획에 대해 아직 확신이 없어요.

english: In my opinion, the {benefit} is worth the cost.
korean: 제 생각에는, 이점이 비용만큼 가치가 있어요.

english: From my {perspective}, this approach makes more sense.
korean: 제 관점에서는, 이 접근법이 더 말이 돼요.

english: It {depends} on the situation we are in.
I {believe} we can find a better solution.
korean: 더 나은 해결책을 찾을 수 있다고 믿어요.

english: That's a good {point}, but I see it differently.
I {agree} with you to some extent.
I {disagree} because the data shows the opposite.
korean: 데이터가 반대를 보여주기 때문에 동의하지 않아요.

english: Could you {clarify} what you mean by that?
korean: 그 말이 무슨 뜻인지 명확히 해줄 수 있어요?

english: Let me {summarize} the main points so far.
We should {compare} the two options carefully.
There are clear {advantages} to working from home.
One {disadvantage} is that you feel lonely.
korean: 한 가지 단점은 외로움을 느낀다는 점이에요.

english: Let's weigh the pros and {cons} together.
I {tend} to agree with the first speaker.
It {seems} that the meeting will be delayed.
{Apparently}, the office will be closed tomorrow.
korean: 듣자 하니 내일 사무실이 문을 닫는대요.

english: {Obviously}, we need more time to decide.
korean: 분명히 우리는 결정할 시간이 더 필요해요.

english: I {definitely} support this proposal.
{Although} it rained, we still went hiking.
korean: 비가 왔지만 우리는 그래도 등산을 갔어요.

english: {Even though} she was tired, she finished the report.
{However}, there is one important issue we missed.
korean: 하지만 우리가 놓친 중요한 쟁점이 하나 있어요.

english: On the other {hand}, this method takes more time.
korean: 반면에 이 방법은 시간이 더 걸려요.

english: {Moreover}, the price has gone up this year.
{Therefore}, we have to change our plan.
As a {result}, sales increased by ten percent.
{Nevertheless}, the team kept working hard.
korean: 그럼에도 불구하고 팀은 계속 열심히 일했어요.

english: We should leave early {otherwise} we will be late.
korean: 일찍 출발해야 해요, 그렇지 않으면 늦어요.

english: {Specifically}, I'd like to talk about the budget.
I {might} go to the concert this weekend.
She {may} not come to the meeting tomorrow.
korean: 그녀는 내일 회의에 안 올 수도 있어요.

english: It {could} rain later, so bring an umbrella.
korean: 나중에 비가 올 수도 있으니 우산을 가져가세요.

english: {Hopefully} the package will arrive by Friday.
We are {probably} going to expand to Asia.
It's {likely} to snow tonight.
korean: 오늘 밤 눈이 올 가능성이 높아요.

english: I {expect} the results next week.
Take a charger, in {case} your battery dies.
korean: 배터리가 다 나갈 경우에 대비해 충전기를 챙기세요.

english: {Unless} we hurry, we will miss the train.
We can stay outside as long {as} it doesn't rain.
korean: 비가 안 오는 한 우리는 밖에 있을 수 있어요.

english: I'm not sure {whether} I should accept the offer.
If I {had} more time, I would learn another language.
korean: 시간이 더 있다면 또 다른 언어를 배울 텐데요.

english: If I were you, I would {apply} for the job.
If the weather is good, we'll have a {picnic}.
korean: 날씨가 좋으면 우리는 소풍을 갈 거예요.

english: Have you {ever} traveled to Europe?
I have {never} eaten that kind of food.
korean: 그런 종류의 음식을 먹어본 적이 없어요.

english: She has {visited} Japan three times.
I {used} to play the guitar when I was younger.
We {would} often visit our grandparents on weekends.
korean: 우리는 주말마다 종종 조부모님을 뵈러 가곤 했어요.

english: I've {already} finished the assignment.
Have you finished the report {yet}?
I {just} got back from a business trip.
I have lived here {since} 2019.
We have been friends {for} ten years.
She has been working on this project {for} months.
korean: 그녀는 몇 달 동안 이 프로젝트를 진행해 왔어요.

english: I'm getting {used} to my new job.
It took a while to {get} used to the time zone.
korean: 시차에 익숙해지는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸어요.

english: Do you have any plans for the {weekend}?
I'm going to {meet} a client at three.
We're {planning} a trip to Spain next summer.
korean: 내년 여름에 스페인 여행을 계획하고 있어요.

english: I'm looking {forward} to seeing the new movie.
I can't wait to {try} the new restaurant.
We're {about} to leave the office.
I have a meeting at nine, then {lunch} with my boss.
korean: 9시에 회의 있고, 그 다음에 상사와 점심이 있어요.

english: She's giving a {presentation} this afternoon.
korean: 그녀는 오늘 오후에 발표를 할 거예요.

english: The {deadline} for this report is Friday.
Can we {reschedule} the meeting to next week?
korean: 회의를 다음 주로 미룰 수 있을까요?

english: My {colleague} will help me with the slides.
I have to {hand} in this assignment by tomorrow.
Could you {follow} up with the client?
I'm working on a new {project} this month.
korean: 이번 달에 새 프로젝트를 진행 중이에요.

english: I really need a {break} this afternoon.
korean: 오늘 오후에 정말 쉬는 시간이 필요해요.

english: My {workload} has been heavy this week.
She got a {promotion} after two years.
We're {hiring} a new designer next month.
He decided to {quit} his job last week.
Could you {fill} out this application form?
I have a job {interview} this Thursday.
The position {requires} three years of experience.
I'm {supposed} to start at nine in the morning.
Please let me {know} if you need anything.
I'd {appreciate} your help with this.
korean: 이 일에 대한 도움을 정말 감사하게 생각할 거예요.

english: Thanks for the quick {response}.
I {apologize} for the inconvenience.
Would you {mind} if I asked a question?
Could I {borrow} your laptop for a minute?
korean: 잠시 노트북 좀 빌릴 수 있을까요?

english: I'd {rather} stay home tonight.
korean: 오늘 밤은 차라리 집에 있는 게 좋겠어요.

english: You'd {better} take an umbrella with you.
You {should} talk to your supervisor about it.
korean: 그 일에 대해 상사와 얘기해 봐야 해요.

english: I {prefer} coffee to tea in the morning.
korean: 아침에 저는 차보다 커피를 더 좋아해요.

english: I'm really {into} cooking these days.
We {hang} out every Friday night.
Let's {catch} up over coffee soon.
korean: 곧 커피 마시면서 얘기 좀 해요.

english: I want to {keep} in touch with my old friends.
We get {along} really well with our neighbors.
He {broke} up with his girlfriend last month.
They {made} up after a long argument.
I {look} after my younger sister on weekends.
Please take {care} of my plants while I'm away.
korean: 제가 없는 동안 식물 좀 돌봐주세요.

english: I've been {dealing} with a lot of stress lately.
I'm so {grateful} for everything you've done.
korean: 당신이 해준 모든 일에 정말 감사해요.

english: I felt really {disappointed} with the result.
I'm a bit {confused} about the instructions.
I was so {embarrassed} when I forgot her name.
korean: 그녀의 이름을 잊었을 때 너무 부끄러웠어요.

english: I'm so {relieved} the exam is over.
She was {impressed} by his presentation.
I'm feeling {anxious} about the interview.
He looks really {stressed} this week.
korean: 그는 이번 주에 정말 스트레스 받아 보여요.

english: I {regret} not studying harder last year.
korean: 작년에 더 열심히 공부하지 않은 게 후회돼요.

english: She felt {guilty} after lying to her friend.
I was {shocked} when I heard the news.
His {annoying} habit is talking too loudly.
korean: 그의 짜증나는 습관은 너무 크게 말하는 거예요.

english: Calm {down}, everything will be fine.
Cheer {up}, things will get better.
Don't {give} up so easily.
I want to {explore} the old town tomorrow.
Where can I buy a {souvenir} for my family?
korean: 가족에게 줄 기념품을 어디서 살 수 있어요?

english: This is a famous {landmark} in the city.
I need to apply for a {visa} before I go.
Please show me your {boarding pass} here.
I'll pick up my bags at the {baggage claim}.
korean: 수하물 찾는 곳에서 가방을 찾을 거예요.

english: I have a long {layover} in Hong Kong.
I'd like a {round-trip} ticket, please.
What's the {exchange rate} for dollars today?
I want to {withdraw} some cash from the ATM.
My bank {balance} is getting low.
I can't {afford} that car right now.
korean: 지금은 그 차를 살 여유가 없어요.

english: Could you {lend} me some money until Friday?
korean: 금요일까지 돈 좀 빌려줄 수 있어요?

english: I have to {pay} the rent by the first.
I plan to {save} up for a new laptop.
korean: 새 노트북을 사기 위해 저축할 계획이에요.

english: The {fee} for the service is quite high.
I forgot to pay my phone {bill}.
This insurance {policy} covers travel accidents.
korean: 이 보험 정책은 여행 사고를 보장해요.

english: We're trying to stick to our monthly {budget}.
The package will be {delivered} tomorrow.
Free {shipping} is included with this order.
korean: 이 주문은 무료 배송이 포함돼 있어요.

english: I'd like to {track} my order, please.
This item is currently {out} of stock.
Does this product come with a {warranty}?
The customer {reviews} are mostly positive.
I usually {check} ratings before I buy anything.
korean: 보통 뭔가 사기 전에 평점을 확인해요.

english: We need to {schedule} a meeting next week.
korean: 다음 주에 회의 일정을 잡아야 해요.

english: Please {set} a deadline for this task.
Let me {check} my calendar first.
I'll {follow} up on this by email.
Could you {confirm} the time of the meeting?
korean: 회의 시간을 확인해 주실 수 있어요?

english: I'm sorry, I have to {cancel} our appointment.
Let's {move} the meeting to two o'clock.
Could we {join} the call from a different room?
I {missed} the last train home.
The flight has been {delayed} by two hours.
We are due to {take} off in ten minutes.
Could you {drop} me off at the station?
My friend will {pick} me up at the airport.
korean: 친구가 공항으로 저를 데리러 올 거예요.

english: We had to {get} off at the wrong station.
Could you {speak} more slowly, please?
I didn't quite {understand} your point.
Could you {repeat} that, please?
I think there's been a {misunderstanding}.
Let me {explain} what I meant earlier.
korean: 제가 아까 무슨 말을 한 건지 설명할게요.

english: Could you {translate} this into English?
Her {pronunciation} is very clear.
I want to be more {fluent} in English.
I'm trying to expand my {vocabulary}.
Practice helps me communicate {better}.
korean: 연습은 제가 더 잘 의사소통하도록 도와줘요.

english: She has a strong American {accent}.
korean: 그녀는 강한 미국식 억양을 갖고 있어요.

english: I keep a {journal} to improve my writing.
The article {mentions} several interesting points.
korean: 그 기사는 흥미로운 점들을 여럿 언급해요.

english: I'd like to {comment} on the new policy.
korean: 새 정책에 대해 의견을 드리고 싶어요.

english: My boss {praised} me for the presentation.
I received {positive} feedback on my report.
She {encouraged} me to apply for the position.
korean: 그녀는 제가 그 자리에 지원하도록 격려했어요.

english: The criticism was hard, but {fair}.
I {support} the new recycling program.
Many people {oppose} the proposed changes.
Let's {compare} the costs and benefits.
There is a big {difference} between the two options.
korean: 두 선택지 사이에는 큰 차이가 있어요.

english: The new model is {similar} to the old one.
In {contrast}, the second option saves money.
korean: 반대로 두 번째 선택지는 돈을 아껴줘요.

english: Sales {increased} by twenty percent last quarter.
Prices have {decreased} over the past year.
korean: 지난 한 해 동안 가격이 감소했어요.

english: The company plans to {expand} into new markets.
We should {reduce} our daily expenses.
Pollution is a serious {issue} in big cities.
We need a creative {solution} to this problem.
Climate change has a huge {impact} on farming.
korean: 기후 변화는 농업에 큰 영향을 미쳐요.

english: What was the main {cause} of the accident?
Eating well has many positive {effects}.
korean: 잘 먹는 것은 긍정적인 효과가 많아요.

english: She came to a different {conclusion} from mine.
I have a {headache} and a sore throat.
The doctor gave me a {prescription} for pain.
There are some side {effects} from this medicine.
korean: 이 약에는 몇 가지 부작용이 있어요.

english: It takes time to {recover} from the flu.
I'm {feeling} better than I did yesterday.
She had {surgery} on her knee last month.
Could you call an {ambulance}, please?
I would {rather} not talk about it now.
korean: 지금은 그 얘기를 안 하고 싶어요.

english: He no {longer} works at the same company.
korean: 그는 더 이상 같은 회사에서 일하지 않아요.

english: I haven't seen her for a {while}.
She won three games in a {row} yesterday.
korean: 그녀는 어제 세 경기를 연속으로 이겼어요.
